cot_prefix: Okay, I need to figure out the best title for this passage about the London Olympics. Let me read through the article again to understand the main points. The article starts by saying that while opinions vary on whether the London Olympics were a success, one certainty is that London hosted the "greenest" Games ever. The key theme here is the environmental efforts made by London. The next paragraphs discuss various green initiatives. They mention cleaning up polluted areas by removing contaminated soil and tearing down old factories. A large percentage of debris was reused for building the Olympic Park, with specific examples like the steel tubes from old gas pipelines used in the stadium's roof. Green building techniques were used in all venues, such as the lightest Olympic Stadium ever built and energy-efficient lighting in the handball field. The article also talks about waste management, using rainwater and recycled wastewater to save water, and converting food waste into renewable energy. Public transport was encouraged to reduce carbon emissions, with trains used for delivering materials instead of cars. Additionally, the anti-doping efforts were highlighted as contributing to the "greenness" of the Games, with only one positive test thanks to advanced testing devices. Now, looking at the options: A) How to deal with the anti-doping – This is mentioned, but it's just one part of the overall green efforts. The main focus isn't solely on anti-doping. B) How to use recycled materials – This is a significant part of the article, but again, the passage covers more than just recycling materials. There are other green initiatives like building techniques, water conservation, public transport, and anti-doping. C) London's green games – This seems to encapsulate the overall theme. The article is about the environmental efforts and achievements of the London Olympics, which are referred to as the "greenest" Games ever.
